methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $475M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Unconventional AI, a Seed-stage company in Chips & Semiconductors founded by Naveen Rao, has raised $475M to date. The relevant comp universe spans 3 public semiconductor players (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, and Etched, though direct same-stage comparables remain sparse in this capital-intensive sector.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given available data. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage, using the $475M total raised as a valuation proxy. This yields a median implied valuation of $1.2B. A ±15% sensitivity band around this anchor—reflecting uncertainty in the multiplier itself—produces a low of $1.0B and high of $1.4B.

Peer-Median triangulation was inapplicable; while 14 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 meet narrow filters for stage, tier, or both. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be executed absent the company's revenue, which private firms rarely disclose. The analysis therefore rests on a single method and carries material uncertainty around (i) the true stage-to-stage step-up multiple for frontier chip ventures and (ii) whether $475M raised represents a meaningful valuation proxy at Seed. The $1.0B–$1.4B range should be weighted toward the low end absent additional disclosed financials or a denser peer set.
